sourceGroups.name: Official Anthropic Sources
sourceGroups.summary: Documentation and guides published by Anthropic.

sourceGroups.name: Practitioner Sources
sourceGroups.summary: Articles, posts, and guides from practitioners using Claude Code.

sourceGroups.name: Interviews and Talks
sourceGroups.summary: Recorded conversations and presentations about AI-assisted development.

sources.title: Claude Code Documentation
sources.author: Anthropic
sources.summary: Official documentation for Claude Code CLI, covering installation, configuration, CLAUDE.md, hooks, MCP, and permissions.
sources.relevance: Primary reference for all levels. Defines the tool capabilities that underpin the entire framework.

sources.title: Prompt Engineering Guide
sources.author: Anthropic
sources.summary: Anthropic's guide to effective prompting techniques for Claude models.
sources.relevance: Directly relevant to Levels 1-2 task framing and context management dimensions.

sources.title: Model Context Protocol Specification
sources.author: Anthropic
sources.summary: The open specification for MCP, defining how AI models connect to external tools and data sources.
sources.relevance: Core reference for Level 3 environment design and MCP server configuration.

sources.title: Claude Agent SDK
sources.author: Anthropic
sources.summary: SDK for building custom agents using Claude, relevant to multi-agent orchestration patterns.
sources.relevance: Key reference for Level 4 workflow engineering and multi-agent design.

sources.title: Anthropic Safety Practices
sources.author: Anthropic
sources.summary: Anthropic's published research and practices around AI safety.
sources.relevance: Foundation for Level 5 safety and governance dimension.

sources.title: Claude Code Best Practices
sources.author: Anthropic
sources.summary: Anthropic's guide to getting the most out of Claude Code, including tips for effective prompting, project setup, and workflow patterns.
sources.relevance: Practical guidance spanning Levels 1-3, particularly task framing and environment design.

sources.title: Patterns for AI-Assisted Development
sources.author: Simon Willison
sources.summary: Ongoing practitioner observations on effective patterns for working with AI coding tools, including prompt strategies and verification approaches.
sources.relevance: Real-world practitioner perspective on Levels 1-3 skills, particularly verification discipline and task framing.

sources.title: AI Coding Workflow Observations
sources.author: Thorsten Ball
sources.summary: A software engineer's detailed observations on integrating AI coding assistants into daily development workflows.
sources.relevance: Informs Level 2-3 workflow patterns and the gap between basic usage and structured collaboration.

sources.title: Emerging Practices for Agentic Coding
sources.author: Various practitioners
sources.summary: Collected community patterns for multi-agent coding workflows, CI integration, and governance approaches emerging from early adopters.
sources.relevance: Informs Levels 4-5 workflow scaling and governance concepts drawn from practitioner experience.
sources.notes: Aggregated from multiple community sources including GitHub discussions, blog posts, and forum threads.

sources.title: Building with Claude: Developer Perspectives
sources.author: Anthropic Developer Relations
sources.summary: Presentations and developer sessions on building effective applications with Claude, including agentic patterns and tool use.
sources.relevance: Provides context for Level 3-4 concepts around tool integration, MCP usage, and agent orchestration.

sources.title: AI Engineering Summit Presentations
sources.author: Various speakers
sources.summary: Conference talks covering the emerging discipline of AI engineering, including governance, safety, and organizational adoption of AI coding tools.
sources.relevance: Informs Level 5 organizational strategy and the broader context of AI coding tool adoption.
sources.notes: Multiple sessions across AI engineering conferences addressing governance and team adoption patterns.
